title: Implement shader-based vertex displacement
summary: Replace sine-based layeredNoise with simplex noise FBM in pointCloud and ribbonField shaders, migrate particleField from CPU-side PointsMaterial to GPU ShaderMaterial with curl-noise displacement, ensuring all three geometry systems use shader-based 3D deformation driven by bass (macro), treble (fine-grain), and time evolution.

currentState.pointCloud: Uses ShaderMaterial with pointWarp.vert.glsl. Has 21+ uniforms including uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, uTime. Contains layeredNoise() function using sine-hash combinations with uNoiseOctaves (1-3). Deformation is additive sine offsets — not coherent spatial noise.
currentState.ribbonField: Uses ShaderMaterial with ribbonWarp.vert.glsl. Mirrors pointCloud pipeline with identical layeredNoise() and uniform set. Has ribbon-specific bass sway logic.
currentState.particleField: Uses PointsMaterial with CPU-side per-frame position/velocity/color loops in draw() (lines 113-181). No ShaderMaterial. Receives only maxParticles and enableSparkle from quality config — missing noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation.
currentState.qualitySystem: Three-tier system in quality.ts controlling noiseOctaves (1-3), pointerRepulsion, slowModulation, and particle/point counts. Already wired for pointCloud and ribbonField but not fully for particleField.
currentState.shaderFiles: Four GLSL files: pointWarp.vert.glsl (134 lines), pointWarp.frag.glsl (21 lines), ribbonWarp.vert.glsl (133 lines), ribbonWarp.frag.glsl (21 lines). No shared noise utility.

steps.title: Create shared simplex noise GLSL module
steps.description: Create src/visual/shaders/noise3d.glsl containing a 3D simplex noise function (snoise) and an FBM wrapper (fbm3) that layers octaves with configurable count. Use the Ashima/webgl-noise MIT-licensed simplex implementation as the base primitive. The fbm3 function should accept position, octave count, lacunarity, and gain parameters.
details: snoise(vec3) returns float in [-1, 1] — classic 3D simplex, ALU-only, no texture lookups
fbm3(vec3 pos, int octaves, float lacunarity, float gain) sums snoise octaves with frequency doubling and amplitude decay
Default lacunarity=2.0, gain=0.5 matches standard FBM
Octave count driven by int uniform (maps to quality tier's noiseOctaves 1-3)
Include curl3(vec3) helper that computes curl of fbm3 field via finite differences — needed by particleField in step 4

steps.title: Upgrade pointWarp.vert.glsl to simplex FBM displacement
steps.description: Replace the existing layeredNoise() function in pointWarp.vert.glsl with an import of noise3d.glsl's fbm3. Wire the noise import in pointCloud.ts by prepending the noise3d.glsl source to the vertex shader string via Vite's ?raw import. Stack fbm3-based displacement on top of existing twist/pointer/breathing deformations. Bass scales low-frequency FBM amplitude for macro 3D displacement; treble scales high-frequency snoise for fine-grain jitter and point size modulation. Time offset drifts the noise domain for continuous evolution.
details: Remove layeredNoise() function entirely from pointWarp.vert.glsl

Replace slow modulation block with: vec3 noisePos = pos * uNoiseFrequency + vec3(t * 0.00008); float bassDisp = fbm3(noisePos, uNoiseOctaves, 2.0, 0.5) * uBassEnergy * 0.4 * ma; pos += normal_approx * bassDisp;
Add treble fine-grain: float trebleDisp = snoise(pos * 3.0 + t * 0.0003) * uTrebleEnergy * 0.12 * ma; pos += vec3(trebleDisp);
Modulate gl_PointSize by: 1.0 + uTrebleEnergy * snoise(pos * 5.0) * 0.3
Time drift in noise domain (t * 0.00008) ensures deformation evolves continuously — not static modulation
In pointCloud.ts: import noise3dSrc from '../shaders/noise3d.glsl?raw' and prepend to vertexShader string before compilation
Existing uNoiseOctaves uniform already wired — no new uniforms needed for octave control

steps.title: Upgrade ribbonWarp.vert.glsl to simplex FBM displacement
steps.description: Mirror step 2 changes into ribbonWarp.vert.glsl. Replace layeredNoise() with fbm3 from noise3d.glsl. Wire noise import in ribbonField.ts. Preserve ribbon-specific bass sway while adding coherent spatial noise displacement. Same bass/treble/time evolution pattern as pointCloud.
details: Remove layeredNoise() function entirely from ribbonWarp.vert.glsl
Same fbm3 displacement pattern as step 2, stacked after ribbon-specific bassSway
Preserve existing ribbonPhase bass sway logic — it adds character specific to ribbons
In ribbonField.ts: import noise3dSrc from '../shaders/noise3d.glsl?raw' and prepend to vertexShader string
Existing uniform set is identical to pointCloud — no changes needed

steps.title: Create particleWarp vertex and fragment shaders
steps.description: Create new ShaderMaterial shaders for particleField. The vertex shader uses curl-noise flow field displacement to preserve the drifting character of the current CPU-based velocity system. Fragment shader matches pointWarp.frag.glsl (soft circular point rendering).
details: particleWarp.vert.glsl: Same uniform interface as pointWarp (all 21+ uniforms) for consistency
Use curl3() from noise3d.glsl for primary displacement — curl noise produces divergence-free flow that mimics the drifting particle behavior
Bass scales curl amplitude for macro 3D displacement: pos += curl3(pos * uNoiseFrequency + vec3(t * 0.0001)) * uBassEnergy * 0.35 * ma
Treble drives high-frequency snoise displacement + point size modulation
Time offset drifts curl field for continuous evolution
Include pointer repulsion (quality-gated), breathing, and twist logic matching pointCloud pattern
Compute vColor via HSL→RGB in vertex shader (matching pointCloud pattern) — replaces CPU color computation
particleWarp.frag.glsl: Can be identical to pointWarp.frag.glsl (soft disc with alpha fade)

steps.title: Migrate particleField.ts from CPU to ShaderMaterial
steps.description: Rewrite particleField.ts to use ShaderMaterial with particleWarp shaders instead of PointsMaterial with CPU loops. Set basePositions once as a buffer attribute. Move all per-frame position and color computation to the GPU. Wire noise3d.glsl import. Update main.ts to pass full quality config (noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation).
details: Replace PointsMaterial with ShaderMaterial using particleWarp.vert/frag
Import noise3dSrc from '../shaders/noise3d.glsl?raw' and prepend to vertex shader
Remove CPU per-frame position loop (lines 113-167) — all displacement now in GPU shader
Remove CPU per-frame color loop (lines 168-177) — color now computed in vertex shader via HSL→RGB
Set basePositions once as BufferGeometry position attribute (no per-frame updates)
Add per-point attributes matching pointCloud: aSize, aHueOffset, aRandom
Add full uniform set matching pointCloud (21+ uniforms including uNoiseOctaves, uEnablePointerRepulsion, uEnableSlowModulation)
draw() method updates uniforms only — no array iteration
Update main.ts (lines 93-96) to pass noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation to createParticleField alongside existing maxParticles and enableSparkle
Use THREE.AdditiveBlending and depthWrite:false to match pointCloud/ribbonField

steps.title: Verification and acceptance criteria checklist
steps.description: Verify all acceptance criteria, Definition of Done items, and non-negotiable requirements are met.
details: AC1: All three geometry systems (pointCloud, ribbonField, particleField) use ShaderMaterial with GPU vertex displacement
AC2: Bass energy drives large-scale 3D displacement via fbm3/curl3 amplitude scaling in all shaders
AC3: Treble energy drives fine-grain snoise displacement and point size modulation in all shaders
AC4: Time-offset noise domain drift ensures deformation evolves continuously — not static
Ticket note: No CPU-based per-point loops remain for deformation
DoD: npm run build succeeds
DoD: No console errors on normal load
DoD: No forbidden storage APIs
DoD: Scene works with and without successful autoplay
DoD: Mute toggle and info overlay still work
Non-negotiable: Perspective camera, volumetric point cloud, shader-based 3D deformation, visible depth/parallax all preserved
Quality tiers: noiseOctaves 1/2/3 gates FBM complexity across all three systems
Reduced motion: uMotionAmplitude 0.2x still applied to all displacement

executionNotes: Step 1 first (shared dependency). Steps 2+3 can be parallelized (independent shader upgrades). Steps 4+5 can be parallelized after step 1 (new shaders + migration). Step 6 is final verification. Noise import wiring happens inline within steps 2, 3, and 5 — no separate wiring step needed.

risks.risk: Simplex noise ALU cost on low-tier devices
risks.mitigation: Quality tier already gates noiseOctaves to 1 on low tier. Single-octave snoise is ~20 ALU ops — comparable to existing sine-hash layeredNoise.

risks.risk: particleField migration breaks existing visual character
risks.mitigation: Curl-noise produces divergence-free flow matching the drifting feel of CPU velocity system. Uniform interface matches pointCloud so all existing quality/evolution knobs apply automatically.

risks.risk: GLSL concatenation (noise3d + shader source) may cause compilation errors
risks.mitigation: Use Vite ?raw import and string concatenation at material creation time. Test on WebGL1 and WebGL2 contexts. Keep noise3d.glsl self-contained with no #version directive.

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.ShaderMaterial deformation: Steps 2, 3, 4, 5 — all three modes use ShaderMaterial
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Bass large-scale 3D displacement: Steps 2, 3, 4 — fbm3/curl3 noise scaled by uBassEnergy
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Treble fine-grain displacement: Steps 2, 3, 4 — high-freq snoise + point size modulation by uTrebleEnergy
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Time evolution: Steps 2, 3, 4 — noise domain offset by uTime drift (t * 0.00008-0.0003)
